name: portable_display_portrait_stand
description: Multi-part PETG portrait stand for a bare portable display on a kitchen bench, with a continuous low front fascia that hides cables and keeps a right-half bottom port keepout clear.

design_intent.silhouette: two rounded cradle feet hidden behind a single continuous low angled front fascia/skirt, with a subtle dress-like flare at the bench and a low rear bridge rail
design_intent.visual_style: soft functional kitchen-bench accessory, rounded and stable without looking like a square bracket
design_intent.surface_continuity: single uninterrupted low front skirt, rounded flared plinths behind it, clean angled support faces, and intentional rear cable-channel interruptions only on the port side
forbidden_features: geometry crossing the right-half bottom port keepout
a split or visually interrupted front skirt
a tall front lip covering the display face
single monolithic part exceeding the Creality K1 bed
cross_sections: side section through either foot shows a 15 degree rear-tilted screen pocket lifted to 32 mm so plug bodies and cable bends clear the bench, with no horizontal flat seat pad in front of the backrest
bottom section through the right half keeps the port edge open and routes cables behind the fascia through a widened local rear-facing 75 mm through-channel with a taller plug bay
plan view shows each printed part inside a 220 x 220 mm Creality K1 bed envelope
design_intent.notes: The generated script owns the exact stand geometry; this brief records the physical contract and manufacturing intent.

requirements: Hold a bare display in portrait orientation at a 15 degree backward tilt.
Use a 12.5 mm padded slot for an 11 mm monitor body plus felt or tape, formed by the lifted front fascia and the angled rear backrest rather than a horizontal shelf.
Use double-sided tape against the rear backrest as the primary screen retention so the front fascia can stay low and angled.
Keep the right-half bottom edge open for ports and route cables rearward behind the fascia through a local port-side tunnel/slot with at least 75 mm of turn depth and 28 mm plug-body clearance.
Extend side-capture fins down to the bench plane so the feet do not contain slicer-visible floating regions.

Add small retrofit skirt spacers that sit on top of the already-printed feet, connect the skirt to the body with tape or adhesive, and carry the monitor short edge at the 32 mm cable-clearance height.
Split the stand into K1-printable PETG parts: left foot, right cable foot, rear bridge, continuous front fascia/skirt, retrofit skirt spacers, and fit coupon.
Export the continuous front fascia/skirt in diagonal print orientation so it fits the 220 x 220 mm Creality K1 bed.
Include a small fit coupon before printing the full assembly.

front_fascia_parameters.purpose: single continuous lifted angled skirt that hides the cable drop from the front while its rear/top edge aligns with the tilted display bottom

notes: No public API, CLI, schema, or workflow change is intended.
No docs needed: one-off generated model and handoff, no repo workflow/API change.
